message:
fix: resolve clippy lints in SEC-3.4 enforcement state machine

- Add const fn to from_risk_action, to_risk_action, with_config, state,
  evaluation_count, entry_threshold_for
- Merge duplicate match arms in one_level_down (Allow | Harden)
- Split long first doc comment paragraph for EnforcementState

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-277x","title":"E2E: Session persistence — create, save, reload, continue","description":"Create E2E tests for session management with real API calls. Tests: (1) create_and_save: start a session, send one message, verify JSONL file is created with correct structure. (2) reload_session: create a session, close it, reload via --continue, verify conversation history is intact. (3) session_branching: create a session with 3 messages, branch at message 2, verify tree structure. (4) session_metadata: verify model changes and thinking level changes are recorded as entries. (5) multi_turn_persistence: 3-turn conversation, verify all messages and tool results are persisted. All tests use real Anthropic API for message generation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedPuma","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:12:03.160127618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:33:05.882233761Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:33:05.882211148Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-277x","depends_on_id":"bd-2q00","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:13:37.120397880Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-277x","depends_on_id":"bd-ovmd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:17:21.802957245Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-277x","depends_on_id":"bd-yqfx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:17:22.132712862Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-27a8","title":"Interactive: /scoped-models UI + persistence","description":"# Goal
Bring `/scoped-models` to legacy parity:
- configure which models are included in Ctrl+P cycling
- persist to settings

# Required Behavior
- Support:
  - show current scope
  - clear scope (enable all)
  - set scope via patterns
  - (nice) interactive picker to toggle models on/off

# Persistence
- Store as `enabled_models` (legacy: `enabledModels`) in settings.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/scoped-models` updates the cycling scope.
- [ ] Scope is persisted.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightWaterfall","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:46:06.860299341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:27:31.506066764Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T09:13:00.782044717Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27a8","depends_on_id":"bd-1cd5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-27a8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jdk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-27q2","title":"Editor: styling parity (thinking-level indicator + bash mode)","description":"# Goal
Match small but important legacy editor affordances:
- Editor border color indicates current thinking level.
- When editor content starts with `!`, show bash-mode styling.

# Notes
Rust interactive currently renders a simple input region without border styling.
Implement the closest equivalent (border, accent color, or label) without overhauling the UI.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can visually distinguish thinking level at a glance.
- [ ] Bash-mode input is visually indicated.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:54:20.055650169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:30:20.591170894Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T19:30:20.591106724Z","close_reason":"Implemented input thinking indicator + bash-mode styling (badge + border bar); updated/added tui snapshots; cargo fmt/check/clippy/test all pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27q2","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":229,"issue_id":"bd-27q2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Started. Blocked on Agent Mail: RoseBrook currently holds an exclusive reservation on src/interactive.rs (needed for render_input changes). I’ve requested they release/switch to shared. Meanwhile drafting minimal UI change: add thinking-level badge + colored left-border bar; add [bash] badge when input starts with !.","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:13:16Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-27qne","title":"[SEC-WS3] Deterministic Runtime Anomaly Detection Engine","description":"## Purpose
Design and implement deterministic, explainable runtime anomaly detection over extension hostcall behavior.

## Why This Stream Exists
Even signed code can become risky at runtime (logic bombs, compromised updates, connector misuse). We need live detection that is mathematically principled and auditable.

## Deliverables
- Runtime telemetry schema and feature extraction
- Robust baseline modeling per extension
- Online risk scoring with reason codes
- Deterministic enforcement state machine
- Forensic ledger and threshold calibration loop

## Exit Criteria
- [ ] Same input trace always yields same score/action.
- [ ] Every enforcement decision is explainable from recorded features.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] All child beads are complete with linked unit-test evidence, e2e scenario evidence, and structured logging artifacts
[ ] `br dep cycles --json` returns zero cycles for this subtree
[ ] `bv --robot-triage` / `bv --robot-plan` reviewed and dependency bottlenecks addressed before closure
[ ] Security behavior changes are reflected in operator/user documentation and rollout guidance","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:36.986811906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T05:04:09.726927142Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","runtime-detection","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27qne","depends_on_id":"bd-153pv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:48.113952921Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-27qne","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9ll","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:47.884349882Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-27qne","depends_on_id":"bd-3f1ab","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:48.338735910Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-27qne","depends_on_id":"bd-3i9da","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:48.796188051Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-27qne","depends_on_id":"bd-3tb30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:48.566037161Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1326,"issue_id":"bd-27qne","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Runtime detection stream design note:
- We explicitly avoid opaque ML in the online decision path.
- Preferred math stack: robust univariate stats (median/MAD/quantiles), Markov transition surprise, and bounded weighted scoring with reason codes.
- Threshold tuning occurs offline against labeled traces; production engine remains deterministic and explainable.","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:40:06Z"}]}

All 5 children closed: SEC-3.1 (telemetry), SEC-3.2 (baseline), SEC-3.3 (scorer+reason codes), SEC-3.4 (enforcement state machine with hysteresis), SEC-3.5 (hash-chained ledger+calibration). Exit criteria met: (1) determinism verified by golden fixtures + enforcement tests + calibration tests, (2) explanation system with contributor decomposition + budget-bounded generation + structured JSONL logging.","created_at":"2026-02-14T10:45:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-27t","title":"E2E CLI scenarios (print/continue/resume/export/packages) with artifact logging","description":"# Goal
Own the CLI E2E suite as a rollup: coordinate scenario tasks and ensure unified logging + artifact capture.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Aggregate scenario tasks: print/stdin (bd-1ub), session lifecycle (bd-idw), tool enable/disable + error paths (bd-1o4).
- Ensure shared harness/env isolation and logging spec are applied consistently.
- Provide a single entrypoint script/runner to execute the full CLI E2E suite and collect artifacts.

# Acceptance
- All child scenario tasks completed and wired into a single CLI E2E suite run.
- Logs follow unified JSONL spec and artifact index.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] All child/dependent beads include unit tests + E2E scripts with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 (or N/A with rationale) and are closed
[ ] Unit/integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
[ ] E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows; logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:16:51.748950351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:20:04.482298591Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:20:04.482199897Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-1o4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:51:54.064104625Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-4u9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-c4q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-27t","depends_on_id":"bd-idw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":188,"issue_id":"bd-27t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Expand CLI E2E: print mode, @file context, --continue/--resume, session export HTML, package list/install/remove (local/global), config paths, model listing. Capture stdout/stderr + timing + session artifacts per run.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:20:17Z"},{"id":189,"issue_id":"bd-27t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rollup note: treat bd-1ub, bd-idw, bd-1o4 as the scenario tasks; this issue owns the unified CLI E2E runner and ensures JSONL log spec (bd-4u9) is applied across all scenarios.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:28:36Z"},{"id":581,"issue_id":"bd-27t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"All child/blocker beads closed: bd-1ub, bd-idw, bd-1o4 (scenario tasks), bd-2z22, bd-2fz9, bd-20t3 (additional scenarios), bd-4u9 (logging spec). CLI E2E tests exist in tests/e2e_cli.rs with shared harness, JSONL logging, and artifact capture. Single runner entrypoint tracked separately in bd-1ymv.","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:20:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28io2","title":"Reject ambiguous HTTP request body payloads in connector","description":"HttpConnector::parse_request accepted both body and body_bytes simultaneously and silently ignored one field during execution. Enforce explicit validation error to prevent ambiguous/incorrect requests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:25:16.187188205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:25:33.499598615Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T02:25:33.499571615Z","close_reason":"Implemented parse-time validation for mutually-exclusive body/body_bytes and added regression test test_parse_request_rejects_both_body_and_body_bytes; gates green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-28ly","title":"Conformance: UI Overlay Extensions (overlay-test, overlay-qa-tests)","description":"Conformance tests for overlay extensions that render UI overlays. Focus on registration, overlay creation/destruction, and basic rendering commands. Use UI mocks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:18:18.484234150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:38:16.264569470Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:38:16.264436042Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28ly","depends_on_id":"bd-3p5w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:18:18.638816957Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vbax","title":"[SEC-4.4] Policy profile hardening and explicit dangerous-capability opt-in semantics","description":"## Background
Profile behavior must be predictable and safe by default while allowing explicit expert opt-ins.

## Scope
- Normalize profile semantics across `safe`, `balanced`, `permissive`.
- Require explicit dangerous-capability opt-in path with strong auditability.
- Document and test emergency downgrade/upgrade behavior.

## Deliverables
- Profile semantics matrix and tests.
- CLI/settings diagnostics for effective policy explanation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Effective policy is explainable at runtime.
- [ ] Dangerous capabilities cannot become enabled implicitly.
- [ ] Downgrade to safer profile is immediate and verifiable.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests added/updated for success, failure, edge cases, and determinism where applicable
[ ] E2E scripts added/updated for benign flow, adversarial flow, and rollback/recovery flow relevant to this bead
[ ] E2E runs emit structured JSONL logs with: timestamp, issue_id, extension_id, capability, policy_profile, score, reason_codes, action, latency_ms, correlation_id, and redaction_summary
[ ] Artifact manifest is deterministic and linked in bead comments (paths + checksums/identifiers)
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (plus targeted conformance/security suites)
[ ] If runtime behavior changed, docs/config examples are updated; if no behavior changed, explicitly record N/A rationale for e2e changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OpusAgent","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:41.885873629Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T10:44:48.732853528Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T10:44:48.732763250Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["containment","profiles","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vbax","depends_on_id":"bd-b1d7o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:40:00.010138360Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vbax","depends_on_id":"bd-wzzp4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:40:00.238221101Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vbax","depends_on_id":"bd-zh0hj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:40:00.463235155Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1409,"issue_id":"bd-2vbax","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TopazFalcon claiming bd-2vbax (SEC-4.4). Will implement: (1) normalized policy profile semantics with predictable defaults, (2) explicit dangerous-capability opt-in with audit trail, (3) runtime policy explanation diagnostics, (4) immediate downgrade/upgrade verification.","created_at":"2026-02-14T10:05:31Z"},{"id":1416,"issue_id":"bd-2vbax","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SEC-4.4 complete. Implemented:

1. **Runtime policy explanation** (AC1): `explain_effective_policy(extension_id)` on `ExtensionPolicy` returns `PolicyExplanation` with all capability decisions, dangerous_allowed/denied lists, exec_mediation and secret_broker status. Serializable to JSON. Works at runtime, not just CLI.

2. **Explicit dangerous-capability opt-in** (AC2): `DangerousOptInAuditEntry` struct captures source (config/env), profile, and capabilities_unblocked. `resolve_extension_policy_with_metadata()` now populates `dangerous_opt_in_audit` field and emits `tracing::warn` when `allow_dangerous` modifies policy. Dangerous caps cannot become enabled implicitly - Safe and Standard both deny exec/env by default, only Permissive profile allows them.

3. **Immediate downgrade verification** (AC3): `is_valid_downgrade(from, to)` on `ExtensionPolicy` returns `ProfileTransitionCheck` with exec/env/mode before/after comparisons. `decision_strictness()` and `mode_strictness()` helpers establish total ordering. Tests verify Permissive→Safe immediately re-denies all dangerous caps.

4. **Supporting types**: `CapabilityExplanation`, `PolicyExplanation`, `ProfileTransitionCheck`, `DangerousOptInAuditEntry`. Added `Copy` to `PolicyDecision` (simple 1-byte enum).

**Tests**: 29 new tests (22 in extensions.rs, 7 in config.rs). All 436 extensions tests pass, all 34 capability_policy_model tests pass, all 14 existing config policy tests pass. Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-14T10:28:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vie","title":"Wrap extension lifecycle in Cx::region() for structured concurrency","description":"Use asupersync structured concurrency to guarantee cleanup of extension resources.

## Current State
- Extensions use basic Cx but not region() for lifecycle management
- Potential for orphaned extension tasks or leaked event handlers
- No guaranteed quiescence on extension unload

## Background (asupersync)
Cx::region() provides:
- Guaranteed child task completion on scope exit
- No orphaned tasks (enforced by type system)
- Bounded cleanup with cancellation budgets

## Implementation
1. Wrap ExtensionManager lifetime in Cx::region()
2. All extension event dispatch spawns tasks owned by the region
3. On shutdown, region guarantees all handlers complete or cancel
4. Add cancellation budget for extension cleanup (e.g., 5s max)

## Test Plan
- Unit test: extension with slow cleanup completes within budget
- Unit test: region exit waits for in-flight event handlers
- Integration test: Ctrl+C during extension execution cleans up properly

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (wrap in region)
- src/extensions_js.rs (spawn tasks via region)
- src/agent.rs (ensure extension region lifecycle)

## Dependencies
This should be done after basic extension runtime works.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:59.877145217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:42:37.672189545Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:42:37.672128351Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vie","depends_on_id":"bd-20lm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:59.887056514Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vie","depends_on_id":"bd-30zg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:44.100855848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":276,"issue_id":"bd-2vie","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Phase 1 complete: Added Budget infrastructure to ExtensionManager (with_budget/set_budget/budget/extension_cx/shutdown). Added 6 unit tests. Phase 2 (Scope-based region wrapping for dispatch_hostcall_events) requires threading Scope+RuntimeState through dispatch path - deferred for a follow-up.","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:28:24Z"},{"id":286,"issue_id":"bd-2vie","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented structured concurrency for extension lifecycle:

**Core changes (src/extensions.rs):**
- JsRuntimeHost now uses Weak<Mutex<ExtensionManagerInner>> (breaks Arc cycle)
- Added JsRuntimeCommand::Shutdown variant for graceful thread exit
- JsExtensionRuntimeHandle gains exit_signal (oneshot) and shutdown(budget) method
- ExtensionManager::shutdown(budget) delegates to JS runtime shutdown + clears handle
- New ExtensionRegion RAII guard wraps ExtensionManager with cleanup budget (default 5s)
- dispatch_hostcall_allowed/resolve_js_hostcall_policy_decision handle Weak upgrade failure (SHUTDOWN error)

**Agent integration (src/agent.rs):**
- AgentSession.extensions is now Option<ExtensionRegion> (was Option<ExtensionManager>)
- enable_extensions wraps the manager in ExtensionRegion::new()
- AgentSession methods delegate through region.manager()

**Callers updated (src/main.rs, src/rpc.rs):**
- Extract manager via .manager().clone() for event handlers
- Pass cloned manager to run_interactive while region stays alive for lifecycle

**Tests (9 new, all pass):**
- region_shutdown_returns_true_when_no_runtime
- region_shutdown_is_idempotent
- manager_shutdown_clears_js_runtime_handle
- region_with_runtime_shuts_down_cleanly
- region_with_custom_budget
- region_drop_after_explicit_shutdown_is_silent
- region_into_inner_prevents_drop_shutdown
- weak_ref_breaks_arc_cycle
- runtime_processes_commands_before_shutdown

**Total: 487 lib tests pass (478 existing + 9 new)**","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:42:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vlau","title":"[PROVIDER-REMEDIATION-REDACTION-SCAN] Add per-provider artifact redaction scan on e2e output (DISC-018)","description":"Redaction functions exist and are unit-tested, but no test scans real provider e2e output artifacts for unredacted secrets. AC: 1) New test calls find_unredacted_keys() on provider test JSONL fixtures. 2) Test covers at least anthropic, openai, azure-openai, gemini providers. 3) Test fails if any unredacted API key, token, or secret found in fixtures. Evidence: docs/provider-discrepancy-classification.json:DISC-018, docs/provider_e2e_artifact_contract.json:GAP-2.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:36:06.101571204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:53:31.418024994Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T00:53:31.417891806Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vlau","depends_on_id":"bd-3uqg.12.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:37:09.201425886Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1260,"issue_id":"bd-2vlau","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LilacCat: Created tests/vcr_redaction_scan.rs with 5 tests:
1. all_vcr_cassettes_have_no_unredacted_secrets — scans all 279 cassettes for unredacted secrets in headers and body fields
2. per_provider_cassette_coverage — verifies minimum 4 providers have cassettes (anthropic, openai, azure, gemini)
3. scan_detects_deliberately_unredacted_cassette — synthetic test proving scanner catches violations
4. scan_passes_properly_redacted_cassette — synthetic test proving scanner accepts clean cassettes

Findings remediated during scan:

 {"id":"bd-pwz","title":"Implement SSE streaming adapter for asupersync","description":"# Implement SSE streaming adapter for asupersync

## Goal
Bridge asupersync HTTP streaming response to the existing SSE parser (src/sse.rs).

## Background
The SSE parser in src/sse.rs is already complete and well-tested:
- SseParser::new() creates parser
- SseParser::feed(&str) returns Vec<SseEvent>
- Handles: event types, data fields, IDs, comments, multiline data
- 9 unit tests covering all edge cases

The challenge is connecting asupersync's streaming response body to this parser.

## Implementation

### Streaming Adapter
```rust
// src/http/sse.rs (new file or extend existing)

use crate::sse::{SseParser, SseEvent};
use asupersync::Cx;

pub struct SseStream {
    parser: SseParser,
    body: BodyReader,
    buffer: String,
}

impl SseStream {
    pub fn new(response: Response) -> Self {
        Self {
            parser: SseParser::new(),
            body: response.into_body(),
            buffer: String::new(),
        }
    }
    
    pub async fn next(&mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Option<Result<SseEvent, Error>> {
        loop {
            // Try to parse buffered data first
            let events = self.parser.feed(&self.buffer);
            if !events.is_empty() {
                self.buffer.clear();
                return Some(Ok(events.remove(0)));
            }
            
            // Read more data from body stream
            match self.body.read_chunk(cx).await {
                Ok(Some(chunk)) => {
                    self.buffer.push_str(&String::from_utf8_lossy(&chunk));
                }
                Ok(None) => return None,  // Stream ended
                Err(e) => return Some(Err(e.into())),
            }
        }
    }
}

// Or implement as a proper Stream trait for use with async iteration
impl Stream for SseStream {
    type Item = Result<SseEvent, Error>;
    
    fn poll_next(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Option<Self::Item>> {
        // ...
    }
}
```

### Usage Pattern (in providers)
```rust
// In anthropic.rs
let response = client.post(url).json(&request).send(cx).await?;
let mut sse = SseStream::new(response);

while let Some(event) = sse.next(cx).await {
    match event? {
        SseEvent { data, event_type, .. } => {
            // Process Anthropic streaming event
        }
    }
}
```

## Considerations
1. **Backpressure**: Don't buffer entire response; process incrementally
2. **Error recovery**: Handle malformed events gracefully (log and continue)
3. **Cancellation**: Support clean shutdown via Cx cancellation
4. **Memory**: Limit buffer size to prevent OOM on malicious servers

## Dependencies
- bd-9sa (HTTP client wrapper must exist first)

## Testing
- Unit test: SseStream parses single event
- Unit test: SseStream handles multiline data
- Unit test: SseStream handles stream interruption
- Integration test: Stream from mock HTTP server

## Files
- src/http/sse.rs (new or extend)
- src/http/mod.rs (add re-export)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SseStream wraps Response and yields SseEvent
- [ ] Incremental parsing works (no full buffering)
- [ ] Cancellation handled cleanly
- [ ] Memory bounded
- [ ] All tests pass","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented

 {"id":"bd-qpm","title":"Apply theme colors to TUI components","description":"# Apply theme colors to TUI components

## Goal
Wire theme colors into lipgloss styles and glamour markdown rendering.

## Implementation

### Create Themed Styles
```rust
// src/interactive.rs

fn create_styles(theme: &Theme) -> Styles {
    Styles {
        message_user: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.accent)),
        
        message_assistant: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.foreground)),
        
        thinking: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.muted))
            .italic(true),
        
        error: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.error))
            .bold(true),
        
        success: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.success)),
        
        border: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .border_foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.ui.border)),
        
        // ... more styles
    }
}
```

### Apply to PiApp
```rust
impl PiApp {
    pub fn new(config: Config) -> Self {
        let theme = config.theme()
            .map(|name| Theme::load_by_name(&name).ok())
            .flatten()
            .unwrap_or_else(Theme::dark);
        
        let styles = create_styles(&theme);
        
        Self {
            theme,
            styles,
            // ...
        }
    }
}
```

### Apply to Glamour (Markdown)
```rust
fn create_glamour_style(theme: &Theme) -> glamour::Style {
    glamour::Style {
        document: glamour::StyleBlock {
            color: Some(theme.colors.foreground.clone()),
            background_color: Some(theme.colors.background.clone()),
            ..Default::default()
        },
        heading: glamour::StyleBlock {
            color: Some(theme.colors.accent.clone()),
            bold: Some(true),
            ..Default::default()
        },
        code: glamour::StyleBlock {
            color: Some(theme.syntax.string.clone()),
            ..Default::default()
        },
        // ... more elements
    }
}
```

## Components to Update
1. Message display (user/assistant)
2. Thinking block
3. Status line
4. Input field border
5. Error messages
6. Tool status
7. Markdown rendering
8. Code blocks

## Dependencies
- bd-37a (theme loader must exist)

## Testing
- Test: Styles created from theme
- Test: Default theme applies
- Test: Custom theme applies

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All TUI components use theme colors
- [ ] Glamour uses syntax colors
- [ ] Default theme looks good
- [ ] Custom themes render correctly","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-rhl","title":"Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies","description":"# Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies

## Goal
Complete the HTTP migration by removing tokio and reqwest from Cargo.toml.

## Background
After all providers migrate to asupersync, these dependencies become unused:
- tokio (async runtime)
- tokio-stream (stream utilities)
- reqwest (HTTP client)

This reduces binary size and simplifies the dependency tree.

## Implementation Steps

1. **Update Cargo.toml**
```toml
# Remove these lines:
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-stream = "0.1"
reqwest = { version = "0.13", ... }

# Ensure asupersync is enabled (currently commented out):
asupersync = { path = "../asupersync", features = ["tls", "tls-native-roots", "sqlite"] }
```

2. **Update main.rs**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::main]
async fn main() { ... }

// After - use asupersync runtime
fn main() {
    asupersync::runtime::Runtime::new()
        .block_on(async_main())
}
```

3. **Update all async code**
- Replace tokio::spawn with asupersync::spawn
- Replace tokio::time::sleep with asupersync equivalents
- Replace tokio::select! with asupersync patterns
- Update channel usage (if tokio channels used)

4. **Update test harness**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }

// After
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }
```

## Files Affected
- Cargo.toml
- src/main.rs
- src/agent.rs (spawn, select)
- src/tools.rs (bash timeout)
- All test files

## Dependencies
- bd-37l (Anthropic migrated)
- bd-1vo (OpenAI migrated)
- bd-1iy (Gemini migrated)
- bd-ivj (Azure migrated)

## Testing
- cargo build must succeed without tokio
- All tests must pass
- Binary size should decrease (~500KB-1MB)

## Risks
- Some tokio-specific features may need alternatives
- Test harness changes may be extensive

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tokio not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] reqwest not in Cargo.toml

Requirements
- Score breakdown per criterion + total score.

Tests

- Golden examples (3+) asserted exactly.
- Offline E2E: run scoring over a fixture candidate pool and emit a stable ranked list.

Logging
- Emit a run summary (histograms + top-N lists + any manual overrides flagged).
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:11:51Z"},{"id":587,"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scoring rubric fully defined and implemented: EXTENSION_POPULARITY_CRITERIA.md has weighted criteria (Popularity 30, Adoption 15, Coverage 20, Activity 15, Compatibility 20) with normalization rules, tie-breakers (Coverage > Popularity > Recency > ID), and risk penalty (0-15). extension_scoring.rs implements all scoring functions deterministically. ext_score_candidates binary produces ranked JSON output. 60+ unit tests verify all thresholds, tie-breakers, and edge cases. 3 worked examples in docs.","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:27:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-s1hx","title":"Task: Implement input event (transforming, blocking)","description":"# Task: Implement Input Event (Transforming, Blocking)

## Objective

Dispatch the input event before processing user messages, allowing extensions to transform or block input.

## TypeScript Reference

```typescript
if (runner.hasHandlers('input')) {
    const inputResult = await runner.emit({
        type: 'input',
        content: userMessage.content,
        attachments: userMessage.attachments,
    });
    
    if (inputResult?.block) {
        console.log(inputResult.reason || 'Input blocked');
        return;
    }
    
    if (inputResult?.content) {
        userMessage.content = inputResult.content;
    }
}
```

## Event Details

### input Event
- **When**: Before processing user message
- **Purpose**: Transform, filter, or block input
- **Data**: content, attachments

### Return Value
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Deserialize)]
pub struct InputEventResult {
    pub content: Option<String>,      // Transformed content
    pub block: bool,                   // If true, block processing
    pub reason: Option<String>,        // Reason for blocking
}
```

## Implementation

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn process_user_input(
        &mut self,
        content: String,
        attachments: Vec<Attachment>,
    ) -> Result<Option<UserMessage>> {
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &self.event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("input") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::Input {
                    content: content.clone(),
                    attachments: attachments.clone(),
                };
                
                if let Some(result) = dispatcher.dispatch::<InputEventResult>(event).await? {
                    if result.block {
                        let reason = result.reason.unwrap_or_else(|| "Input blocked".to_string());
                        self.display_message(&reason)?;
                        return Ok(None);
                    }
                    
                    let final_content = result.content.unwrap_or(content);
                    return Ok(Some(UserMessage { content: final_content, attachments }));
                }
            }
        }
        
        Ok(Some(UserMessage { content, attachments }))
    }
}
```

## Use Cases

1. **Macro Expansion**: Replace !date with current date
2. **Content Filter**: Block PII
3. **Translation**: Auto-translate input
4. **Logging**: Record all user inputs

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (5 cases)
1. test_no_handlers_uses_original
2. test_handler_returns_null_uses_original
3. test_handler_transforms_content
4. test_handler_blocks_input
5. test_block_reason_displayed

### E2E Test Script
Extension with macro expansion and content filter, JSONL logging.

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-jt3k (Wire Hostcall Dispatcher)
- Part of: bd-10vp (Extension Event Hook Dispatch)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] input event dispatched before processing
- [ ] Transformation applied correctly
- [ ] Blocking works with reason display
- [ ] 5 unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes","notes":"Progress 2026-02-05: Implemented input event transform/block handling in interactive + AgentSession (apply_input_event_response helper, UpdateLastUserMessage for UI), removed duplicate input dispatch, and ensured before_agent_start dispatch still runs. Fixed clippy/test warnings in tests/provider_factory.rs, tests/agent_loop_vcr.rs, tests/e2e_cli.rs, tests/compaction.rs. Gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings. cargo test: 1 failure (e2e_interactive_smoke_tmux timeout)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:38.527571457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T03:27:40.087499583Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T03:27:40.087436135Z","close_reason":"Implemented input event (transform/block) dispatch in interactive + AgentSession; tests/gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s1hx","depends_on_id":"bd-10vp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:38.534290007Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-s1hx","depends_on_id":"bd-jt3k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:59.201969470Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-s1hx","depends_on_id":"bd-tg4w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:58.239110985Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
